summary: Apple's new policy allowing app developers to collect user contact information in-app and pitch payments out-of-app will have a much larger impact than most people expect. Game developers, who generate 60% of App Store revenue, are uniquely positioned to exploit this because they already have sophisticated systems for incentivizing user behavior through in-game rewards. Games like Genshin Impact can trivially offer small in-game rewards for providing email addresses, then redirect purchases to web storefronts at a ~10% discount, improving margins from 70% to 85%. McKenzie predicts with 90% confidence that major games will implement this within six weeks, and the ripple effects will extend to SaaS businesses and other platform ecosystems.
key_points: Apple's steering policy change is more significant than it appears because game developers already have the infrastructure to incentivize user behavior changes through in-game reward systems

Games like Genshin Impact can use virtual currency rewards (nearly free to produce) to collect emails and redirect purchases to web storefronts, improving margins by roughly 20% in a single engineering sprint

The gacha model and virtual currency systems give game developers extraordinary leverage to influence payment behavior at massive scale—Genshin Impact alone has transacted over a billion dollars through app stores
Paying users to change transactional behavior is standard practice in payments—credit card rewards programs do exactly this to compete for share of wallet
Apple's decisions on steering will likely influence policies across many other platform ecosystems, as Jobs originally established the 30% take rate as an industry Schelling point
money_quotes: This improves the margin on currency purchases from about 70% to about 85%, at a stroke, which is an incredible 20% lift for one engineering sprint.
Not implementing this costs millions of dollars per week you delay.
Paimon could be employed in a B2B SaaS company's marketing department, and would be crushing her KPIs, because unlike the typical marketer she has literally infinite gold to incentivize uptake with.
The implicit cash value of 20 primogems is approximately 25 cents but they're, of course, free to the Federal Reserve of Primogems.
Some might ask whether it is sporting to pay the user to change their transactional behavior. This is, to put it mildly, not unprecedented in payment ecosystems!

Steve Jobs effectively established a Schelling point that owning a customer relationship in digital goods was worth a 30% take rate, and everyone since has either been on that Schelling point or positioned in direct opposition to it.
All's fair in love, war, and payments.
Every credit card company has whales, too.
key_insight: Apple's seemingly incremental steering policy change will trigger massive payment behavior shifts because game developers already possess the sophisticated reward systems needed to redirect billions in transactions away from app stores.
